Direction: Higher = more women represented among tertiary STEM graduates
Main limitation: Captures graduation output in selected STEM fields. Does not measure later STEM employment, retention in technical careers, or quality of career progression.

Direction: Higher = larger male advantage in youth coding activity
Main limitation: Measures coding activity among 16-24-year-olds, not coding proficiency, formal ICT education, employment in technology, or later labour-market outcomes.

Direction: Higher = larger male advantage in labour-force participation
Main limitation: Measures labour-force participation for ages 15-74, not employment quality, occupation, earnings, seniority, job security, or unpaid care responsibilities.

Direction: Higher = larger wage inequality / worse pay outcome for women
Main limitation: Unadjusted wage gap indicator. Should not be interpreted as equal pay for equal work. May reflect occupational sorting, hours worked, sector, seniority, career interruptions, and other structural differences.

Direction: Higher = women are more concentrated in part-time employment relative to men
Main limitation: Measures the gender difference in part-time employment incidence. Does not distinguish voluntary from involuntary part-time work and does not directly measure unpaid care responsibilities, job quality, precarity, or career penalties.

Direction: Higher = more women represented in senior and middle management positions
Main limitation: Used as a proxy for leadership and labour-market power. Does not measure women on company boards directly and does not capture sector, firm size, decision-making authority, pay, or seniority within management categories.
